criteria.text: An input lets the player enter their name; the entered name is reused on every other screen and persists across reloads
A start button creates a new battle and opens the battle page
The page shows the character's avatar
The page shows the player's name and the win/loss record
The player can choose a new avatar from a set of images, and the chosen avatar is reflected everywhere it's shown
The player can change their name; the new name is reflected on every other screen and persists across reloads
The page shows the player and the opponent with interactive HP bars that update according to the battle logic
When a battle is created, the opponent is picked from a predefined pool of at least 2 opponents with different attack/defense profiles
Full working battle mechanics: zone selection, attack/defense matching, simultaneous resolution, opponent randomization within profile, no repeated zones in a single turn
Critical hits are implemented: a random chance to deal extra damage; a critical hit also breaks through a block
A battle log shows every action of every turn with the required fields (WHO, WHOM, WHERE, HOW MUCH)
Log entries visually highlight key pieces of information (different styles for names, zones, damage values), as shown in the demo

All game data is persisted, so that after a page reload the player can continue with the same character, keep their win/loss record, and resume an in-progress battle in the exact same state (HP, current turn, log)
A fight can end in a single turn because HP is not at least 3x the base damage
A UI framework (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, etc.) is used
